# Questions in Livestream 11

Live Stream #11: Questions at the Eleventh Hour

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07</a> | Are the songs of Miriam and Deborah written in an older form of Hebrew, and can their language help date them to the 11th century BCE? | Falk said poetic sections may preserve older features, but poetry is hard to date securely; Hebrew was also updated over time, so linguistic dating has limits. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:52</a> | Are there any fringe history views that you hold or think are plausible? | Falk said he is generally a conventional, evidence-driven historian and could not think of a fringe history view he would support. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=691" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:31</a> | Will we ever find evidence for a historical Moses outside the Bible? | Probably not. Falk suggested Moses' Egyptian connection would be hard to identify archaeologically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:09</a> | Could anyone besides Joshua and the Israelites have destroyed Jericho and its walls? | Egypt is unlikely in Falk's view, and neighboring city-states are possible but less compelling. He treated the Israelites as the strongest remaining explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:50</a> | Is the Septuagint based on an older Hebrew text, and is it closer to the originals than the Masoretic Text? | Yes to an older Hebrew Vorlage, but not always closer. Falk said textual decisions have to be made verse by verse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:13</a> | Were the Trojans the Sea Peoples after Troy? | Falk treated the Sea Peoples as Greek or Anatolian Greek groups. He thought the Trojans were more likely pressured by them than identical with them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1467" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:27</a> | Is there evidence of a seven-year famine in Egypt, and would that help date the Exodus? | Egypt has many recorded famines across long periods, so famine evidence does not narrowly date the Exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1596" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:36</a> | Did sites like Gobekli Tepe and Catalhoyuk leave a legacy in the ancient Near East, such as excarnation as a precursor to resurrection beliefs? | Falk treated that as possible only in a broad sense and did not argue for a clear direct line. He also noted this is outside his main specialty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:44</a> | Does the Exodus point to an early date for the Pentateuch? | Falk said yes, citing itinerary details plus Egyptian vocabulary, names, and customs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=1996" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:16</a> | What are your thoughts on the "house of kettle" reading of the Tel Dan Stele? | Falk rejected it as ridiculous and said he does not think the Tel Dan Stele reads "house of kettle." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2050" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:10</a> | What evidence places Joseph in Egypt during the 15th rather than the 12th dynasty, and can we rule out chariots in the 12th dynasty? | Falk argued chariot warfare fits the 15th dynasty much better and said the 12th dynasty lacks the horse-breeding and other evidence he would expect. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2287" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:07</a> | Were there two Judean campaigns by Sennacherib, and why did the Rabshakeh come if Hezekiah had already paid tribute? | Falk said he had not yet looked into that enough to give a firm answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2393" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:53</a> | What books are a good starting point for Middle Kingdom history and culture? | He started with Ian Shaw and mentioned work by Leprohon and Quirke, while noting the Middle Kingdom is not his specialty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:59</a> | Is Pepi II's reported 94-year reign plausible? | Falk treated it as well documented and plausible, though he also argued very long reigns tend to weaken kingdoms politically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2609" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">43:29</a> | What would you recommend for beginners in Egyptian history, including primary texts and general surveys? | Falk recommended Ian Shaw's Oxford handbook/history volume as a strong starting primer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=2743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:43</a> | What realistic evidence could overturn major scholarly opinion on the Exodus or conquest, and could biblical minimalism change its mind? | He expects more ostraca, texts, and excavation data, especially from the Wadi Tumilat and Israel, but says some skeptical settings may resist changing even when evidence improves. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3086" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:26</a> | What should we make of the claim that Joseph's sarcophagus was found near Jacob's Well and taken away by Theodosius II? | Falk said it likely was not Joseph's original sarcophagus and was skeptical of strong conclusions based on later local identifications. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3096" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:36</a> | What is your opinion on identifying Jacob with "Yikabhar"? | Falk said he did not know the name's origin well enough and wanted more context before judging it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:19</a> | Did the Egyptians ever undertake expeditions like the Phoenicians? | Yes. Falk pointed to Egyptian Red Sea expeditions to Punt as a clear example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3562" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:22</a> | Why were the Sea Peoples on the move, and why target Egypt? | Falk tied their movement to food pressure and Late Bronze Age instability. Egypt was a rich but risky target because it was the region's breadbasket. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3757" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:37</a> | Did Amenhotep III refuse a Babylonian king's request to send an Egyptian princess in marriage? | Falk said yes and pointed to the Amarna letters. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=3922" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:22</a> | How would you refute James and van der Veen's theory identifying Zerah the Cushite with an official under Ramesses IV? | Falk had not studied that exact proposal closely, but said van der Veen's broader views are highly idiosyncratic and fringe. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=4078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:58</a> | If Genesis preserves oral history but the Avaris community was polytheistic, who preserved the tradition? | Falk said the sons of Jacob could have preserved it and noted Genesis does not portray the patriarchs as strongly monotheistic in the later sense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=4281" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:21</a> | Is there much more biblically related material still to be found among the Oxyrhynchus texts? | Probably not much more biblical material. Falk thought most of the major biblical finds were likely extracted early. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=4479" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:39</a> | How much Phoenician influence was there on Israel? | Not much in early Israel, but more during the divided monarchy through trade, diplomacy, and royal contacts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=4486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:46</a> | What is your favorite programming language? | Python. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=4721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:41</a> | Any thoughts on the identity of Cushan-Rishathaim? | After a viewer supplied context, Falk connected him with Aram Naharim/Mitanni and a Hurrian setting, while saying there are no historical references for the individual himself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:55</a> | Did the sons of Jacob identify their god with a generic high god like El until Yahweh identified himself to Moses? | Falk leaned yes, preferring El or El Shaddai language before the Mosaic revelation of the divine name. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:31</a> | Did ancient Israel practice chattel slavery in a way similar to U.S. chattel slavery? | No. Falk said ancient Israelite slavery differed significantly from modern race-based chattel slavery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5536" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:16</a> | Could the famine in Ruth roughly correlate with the famines of the Late Bronze Age collapse? | Possibly, because Falk treated the Late Bronze collapse as a long regional crisis rather than a single-year event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5663" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:23</a> | Would Falk debate Petrovich again? | He indicated no interest in another three-way debate under the proposed arrangement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:17</a> | Magic: The Gathering or Dungeons & Dragons? | Falk said he was a Dungeons & Dragons third edition player and enjoyed being dungeon master, but never got into Magic: The Gathering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=5783" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:23</a> | How should Gobekli Tepe affect our understanding of later Egyptian and Canaanite culture? | Falk said very little directly; if there was influence, he saw it as more agricultural than religious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=6016" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:16</a> | Which Candace of Ethiopia did the eunuch of Acts 8 serve under? | Falk said Amanitore seemed the likeliest candidate, while stressing that the Candace chronology is confusing and thinly documented. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=6155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:35</a> | Where did the modern "curse of the pharaohs" myth come from? | Falk said it largely came from a British newspaper reaction to Howard Carter's exclusive arrangement, though some tomb warnings do exist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/nTORoSwXMHQ?t=6420" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:00</a> | Hieroglyphics versus cuneiform: which appears first in the archaeological record? | Falk said they appear very close together in time, within roughly a century, and the evidence does not settle the question cleanly. |
